assertions.text: Agent identifies at least 4 distinct commit groups from the described changes
assertions.evidence: 5 commits: ci, refactor(auth), fix(email), feat(profile), build(frontend)

assertions.text: CI config change gets a ci: type commit
assertions.evidence: Commit 1: ci: add Python 3.12 to test matrix

assertions.text: Auth refactor gets a refactor: type commit (not feat: or fix:)
assertions.evidence: Commit 2: refactor(auth): extract validate_token into utils module

assertions.text: Email bug fix gets a fix: type commit
assertions.evidence: Commit 3: fix(email): handle missing recipient without null pointer

assertions.text: Agent presents the plan and asks for confirmation before committing
assertions.evidence: Response ends with 'Does this look right? Happy to adjust any groupings or commit messages before I execute.'

assertions.text: Agent acknowledges the large changeset and suggests smaller working sessions going forward
assertions.evidence: Opening note: 'This is a large changeset with 15 files across 6 directories. Going forward, it's worth committing more frequently...'

notes: Old skill split package.json + package-lock.json into a separate build(frontend): commit, separate from the profile feature. This is debatable — the skill says 'configuration changes that enable a feature go with that feature', which would suggest bundling them. The with-skill run applied this correctly.
